title: Grandpa Frank Reuben Sandwiches
ingredients: 1 tbsp horseradish (fresh if possible, but not creamed horseradish)
Salt and freshly ground black pepper to taste
instructions: Mix together mayonnaise, ketchup, hot sauce, horseradish, Worcestershire sauce, smoked paprika, fresh chives, and minced garlic in a bowl.
Season the dressing with salt and freshly ground black pepper to taste.
Refrigerate the Russian dressing until ready to use.
Butter the outsides of the marble rye bread slices.
Generously coat the inside of each bread slice with Russian dressing.
Place sliced Swiss cheese on all bread slices (the cheese on both slices serves as glue to hold the sandwich together).
Top one bread slice with drained sauerkraut.
Stack a generous amount of pastrami on top of the sauerkraut.
Top with the other slice of marble rye bread with dressing and Swiss cheese.
Grill the sandwich on both sides until toasted.
Serve with dill pickles and potato chips.
notes: Grandpa Frank's favorite was on marble rye bread and toasted
Best eaten while playing Monopoly and watching football
Use good-quality pastrami, such as Boar's Head brand
Fresh horseradish is preferred over creamed horseradish
The recipe is adapted from Joshua Weissman's Russian Dressing
Consider growing fresh chives in your backyard for the best flavor
